title: Which DVD set should I buy
display_title: Which DVD set should I buy
wikitext:
[]: Which DVD set should I buy?             Quote Post by ungenesis » Tue Sep 25, 2007 3:28 pm I'm ready to purchase my own Eva DVD set. What's the best version to get? Platinum?

[]: Re: Which DVD set should I buy?             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Tue Sep 25, 2007 3:33 pm ungenesis wrote: I'm ready to purchase my own Eva DVD set. What's the best version to get? Platinum?

[]: Re: Which DVD set should I buy?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 25, 2007 3:43 pm ungenesis wrote: Platinum?

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Tue Sep 25, 2007 7:48 pm Question on this topic also Does the new Japanese set they release have english subtitles? just wondering, probably not Personally Im getting the tin It comes out the day before my birthday Im happy about that

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Sep 25, 2007 8:37 pm EvaCub wrote: Does the new Japanese set they release have english subtitles?

[]:             Quote Post by NhouzXM » Tue Sep 25, 2007 10:44 pm I just got the platinum thin pack from Amazon (along with EoE) and i must say, GREAT quality, i coulda used some special features but oh well, the show is more important and the quality is amazing

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Sep 26, 2007 2:09 am NhouzXM wrote: I just got the platinum thin pack from Amazon (along with EoE) and i must say, GREAT quality, i coulda used some special features but oh well, the show is more important and the quality is amazing

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 26, 2007 10:57 am CitizenGeek wrote: So, have the special features been ommitted from the thin pack version, then?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Sep 26, 2007 11:11 am Ornette wrote: CitizenGeek wrote: So, have the special features been ommitted from the thin pack version, then?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 26, 2007 12:29 pm CitizenGeek wrote: That's true, but it was nice to have them all the same!

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Sep 26, 2007 1:21 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: CitizenGeek wrote: That's true, but it was nice to have them all the same!

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Wed Sep 26, 2007 9:08 pm CitizenGeek wrote: Most of the ADV extras? They were misleading and cringe-worthy a lot of the time, but I'd be lying if I said I didn't enjoy them or didn't appreciate their presence. The commentaries with Allison Keith and Spike Spencer were good for me, as I love both of those actors.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Oct 04, 2007 10:28 pm I just Pre-ordered the tin set for $77 Im willing to bet thy'll rerelease it again

[]:             Quote Post by Avatar of Dreams » Thu Oct 04, 2007 10:49 pm EvaCub wrote: I just Pre-ordered the tin set for $77 Im willing to bet thy'll rerelease it again

[]:             Quote Post by NhouzXM » Fri Oct 05, 2007 11:00 pm Avatar of Dreams wrote: EvaCub wrote: I just Pre-ordered the tin set for $77 Im willing to bet thy'll rerelease it again

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sat Oct 06, 2007 7:38 am Some of the extras were really cool, like the clean versions of the opening/ending sequences. But then again I love those regardless of what anime it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Oct 12, 2007 8:20 pm Platinum is probably the best option at the moment, especially cost wise. Renewal is always there, but there is the cost of importing and so fourth. Renewal also doesn't have subtitles . . . so, yeah.

[]: Eva DVDs             Quote Post by Itac » Sun Mar 29, 2009 8:46 pm Okay, so, maybe I'm feeling a little lazy tonight. The thing is, my discs are scratched and not playing well. On top of that, one of them doesn't even play in my dvd player(never has, even brand new.) So I have to ask, what DVD collection is the best out there, for the money? Unfortunately I can't go spending loads on the DVDs, but I do want something nice with the most features, particularly the Directors Cuts if they're available with any of the boxes. Edit: Oh and feel free to chastise me if this is posted elsewhere, like I said I'm feeling lazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Mar 29, 2009 9:04 pm Platinum thinpack (also called platinum collection) contains the renewal series and all the DCs. It is recently out of print (like much of ADV's material), so you might have to look around. If you find it, chances are it'll be in the $40 to $50 range.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sun Mar 29, 2009 9:11 pm Best Buy also has some special Holiday edition of NGE for sale. But it's around $110 USD.
